title: [Seq] Add canonicalization for FirReg with mux-based constant drivers
body:
Implement a canonicalization pattern for seq.firreg operations that optimizes registers with mux-based self-loops to constant values [exactly same way](https://github.com/llvm/circt/blob/5dc12802ef2b8264a00b70f236beae886db2c0da/lib/Dialect/FIRRTL/FIRRTLFolds.cpp#L3095) in FIRRTL dialect. 

The pattern matches registers where the next value is a mux with one branch being the register itself (creating a self-loop) and the other branch being a constant:
```mlir
  %mux = comb.mux %cond, %reg, %constant  // or (%constant, %reg)
  %reg = seq.firreg %mux clock %clk
```
In such cases, the register effectively holds a constant value and can be replaced with that constant, eliminating the register entirely.

The optimization is only applied when:
- One mux branch is the register output (self-loop)
- The other mux branch is a constant
- If the register has a reset value, it must be a constant matching the mux constant
